symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical, sharply demarcated chalk-white (fully depigmented) macules on the dorsal hands

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetry and complete depigmentation in an acral distribution are characteristic of non-segmental vitiligo.
Post-inflammatory change is hypopigmented (lighter), not stark depigmented white, and follows a preceding dermatosis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of scale, active border, itch or pain over the patches

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Dermatophyte infection produces a scaly, often pruritic, active advancing border; its complete absence effectively excludes tinea.
Vitiligo affects pigment only — the epidermal surface is normal, with no scale or inflammation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reactive erythema/soreness of surrounding skin from obsessive washing with hot water and harsh soaps

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hot water plus harsh soaps and repeated sanitiser use is a classic cause of irritant dermatitis; the redness is self-inflicted and reversible.
Redness is non-specific; here it is temporally explained by the washing behaviour, not infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe acute anxiety / panic attack with compulsive hand-washing and catastrophic contagion beliefs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Panic attack, catastrophic misattribution and compulsive washing define the acute psychological problem driving the presentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no systemic illness, no spreading warmth/swelling of the hands

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, spreading warmth and swelling argues strongly against current secondary infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Preserved sensation within the white patches (leprosy screen — patient-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anaesthesia within a patch is the cardinal leprosy sign; reported normal sensation argues against it, but self-report is not equivalent to formal sensation testing.
Vitiligo patches retain normal sensation — consistent, though non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Formal in-person sensation testing and peripheral-nerve palpation within/around the patches

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Definitive exclusion of leprosy requires clinician sensation testing (fine touch/pinprick/temperature) and palpation for thickened nerves — not achievable over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dermatological examination: well-demarcated depigmented macules, intact soft supple skin, no scale/raised border/central clearing, no lymphadenopathy (± Wood's lamp accentuation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sharply demarcated fully depigmented macules with normal surface and Wood's-lamp accentuation are highly characteristic — but this is a clinician exam finding, sealed from the AI Doctor.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs (BP 135/85, HR 95, Temp 36.8, RR 18, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia and modestly raised BP are consistent with the acute anxiety state; afebrile and fully saturated argue against systemic illness. Sealed clinician measurement — the AI infers distress from the patient's report, not a measured vital.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Symmetrical depigmentation + no scale/itch + preserved sensation together point to benign vitiligo and away from infective and neurocutaneous mimics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms depigmentation and lesion characteristics; appropriate at a routine in-person review, not urgently.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Clinical sensation testing and peripheral-nerve palpation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Formal leprosy exclusion; a routine in-person manoeuvre given normal reported sensation and no other stigmata.
Not indicated — there is no scale, itch or active border, so dermatophyte infection is not clinically suspected; over-ordering it endorses the patient's contagion misattribution.
